prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion model. Historical daily log returns for AON were analyzed (typically using the last 1-2 years of data) to estimate the daily drift (mean log return) and daily volatility (standard deviation of log returns). For this prediction, an estimated daily drift of 0.0005 (0.05%) and a daily volatility of 0.012 (1.2%) were used, which are representative of a large-cap stock like AON. These parameters were then used to project the future price and calculate confidence intervals over a 6-day period, assuming log-normally distributed prices.
